metadata.arena_note: Condensed Arena (5 competitors x 2 rounds) per user instruction. Premium branded eCom offer architecture won decisively over traditional DR offer stack. Self-approved — user sleeping per overnight execution protocol.
metadata.format_context: Apple/VanMoof-style premium branded eCom page. NOT traditional DR VSL. NOT long-form sales letter. Physical product with two SKUs, Founders 500 limited edition launch.

core_offer.dream_outcome: Your putting finally matches the rest of your game. The putter carousel stops. Consistent face angle under pressure. Short putts become routine. The score reflects the ball-striking.

dream_outcome_specificity.who: Serious amateur golfers (5-15 handicap) who are strong ball-strikers but weak putters — the GIR-to-score gap persona
dream_outcome_specificity.what: A putter that eliminates equipment-induced face drift through Forward Axis Weighting, so the golfer's technique shows in the result
dream_outcome_specificity.when: Immediately — the physics work from the first putt, no adjustment period
dream_outcome_specificity.how_much: Closing the 3-5 stroke gap between ball-striking handicap and actual handicap

core_offer.core_product: RS1 Putter — the first putter with Forward Axis Weighting that puts 75%+ of the head weight in front of the shaft, reversing the gravitational face drift present in every other putter
core_offer.positioning: Premium engineered instrument (not a training aid, not a gimmick). The third generation of putter design — after traditional putters (Gen 1) and zero-torque putters like LAB Golf (Gen 2). The putter that works WITH gravity instead of against it.

value_stack.description: 360g multi-material putter head with Forward Axis Weighting (75%+ of weight in front 25% of head), steel shaft, rubber grip. 10-piece construction: CNC-machined steel face, aluminum tail, carbon composite crown.
Same 360g Forward Axis Weighted head as Standard, with graphite shaft (lighter, reduces fatigue) and premium PU grip (softer feel, better moisture management).

core_offer.total_stated_value: $399 / $429 (no inflated value stacking — premium branded pricing, not DR value-stack format)

problem_solution_map.problem: Face drift: Rear Axis Weighting pulls the face open during the stroke
problem_solution_map.solution: Forward Axis Weighting moves CG ahead of shaft axis — gravity pulls face toward square
problem_solution_map.delivery: Built into the putter head — 75%+ of 360g in front 25% of head

problem_solution_map.problem: Pressure degradation: grip tightens under pressure, compensation fails
problem_solution_map.solution: Forward Axis Weighting eliminates the need for hand compensation — physics are pressure-independent
problem_solution_map.delivery: Structural — the gravitational torque works identically regardless of grip pressure

problem_solution_map.problem: Practice-to-course transfer gap: results on practice green don't carry to the course
problem_solution_map.solution: The variable that pressure exploits (hand compensation) is removed, so performance transfers
problem_solution_map.delivery: Inherent to the forward CG design — no technique change required

problem_solution_map.problem: Putter carousel: buying new putters every 6-18 months hoping 'this one' is different
problem_solution_map.solution: Addresses the root cause all previous putters shared (Rear Axis Weighting), breaking the cycle
problem_solution_map.delivery: The design solves the common flaw — there is no reason to keep searching

problem_solution_map.problem: Excessive stroke arc: wider arc = more face rotation = more face angle error
problem_solution_map.solution: 74-degree upright lie angle reduces stroke arc, producing a more straight-back-straight-through motion
problem_solution_map.delivery: Built into the putter geometry — 74 degrees vs industry standard ~70 degrees

problem_solution_map.problem: Steering and manipulation: actively guiding the putter face through impact
problem_solution_map.solution: Forward Axis Weighting creates a natural pendulum — heavy end falls forward, gravity does the guiding
problem_solution_map.delivery: The weight distribution creates the pendulum automatically

problem_solution_map.problem: Identity split: 'I'm a good ball-striker who can't putt' — scorecard doesn't match ability
problem_solution_map.solution: When putting equipment stops fighting the golfer, putting results match ball-striking quality
problem_solution_map.delivery: The transformation is the resolution of the identity tension

problem_solution_map.problem: Overthinking: racing mind full of mechanical checkpoints over every putt
problem_solution_map.solution: With face control handled by physics, the mind quiets — only line and speed remain
problem_solution_map.delivery: Cognitive simplification through equipment-handled face control

problem_solution_map.problem: Short putt anxiety: dread and shaking over 3-6 footers, especially in competition
problem_solution_map.solution: Consistent face angle removes the primary source of short putt misses (face drift under pressure)
problem_solution_map.delivery: Face Down Balance ensures face tracks true on the short putts that matter most

problem_solution_map.problem: Multi-material mediocrity: most putters are single-material, limiting weight distribution options
problem_solution_map.solution: 10-piece multi-material construction allows extreme Forward Axis Weighting impossible with traditional design
problem_solution_map.delivery: CNC-machined steel face (heavy where it matters), aluminum tail (light), carbon composite crown (light)

problem_solution_map.problem: Lack of visual proof: golfers cannot verify a putter's claims before purchase
problem_solution_map.solution: Face Down Balance provides a 5-second visual test: place RS1 face-down on a flat surface, face stays down. Every other putter face sits up.
problem_solution_map.delivery: Physical demonstration — repeatable by anyone, anywhere, before or after purchase

problem_solution_map.problem: Premium price with no differentiation: $400+ putters from Scotty Cameron and others offer craftsmanship but no mechanism innovation
problem_solution_map.solution: RS1 offers a provable physics advantage (Forward Axis Weighting) at a price below Scotty Cameron ($449-469) and LAB Golf ($449-499)
problem_solution_map.delivery: Premium engineering at a competitive price point

dream_outcome.rationale: Putting that matches ball-striking ability. Resolves the deepest identity tension in the target audience (the scorecard split). Extremely high dream outcome because it addresses a problem golfers have spent years and thousands of dollars failing to solve.

perceived_likelihood.rationale: The Let It Fall / Face Down Balance demo provides pre-purchase visual proof. The physics are demonstrable. McGinley's credentials authenticate the engineering. However, no independent test data exists yet, so likelihood stops at 8 rather than 9-10.

time_delay.rationale: The physics work from the first putt. No adjustment period, no training required, no habit change needed. The putter handles face control immediately. Fastest possible time to result for a putting improvement product.

effort_sacrifice.rationale: The entire promise is 'Do less. Make more.' The putter requires LESS effort, not more. No technique change, no practice regimen, no video instruction to follow. The sacrifice is only financial ($399-429) — which is less than Scotty Cameron or LAB Golf.

bonuses.name: RS1 Alignment System Quick-Start Card
bonuses.description: Printed card included in the box that shows the optimal alignment method for RS1's unique geometry — how the upright lie angle and Forward Axis Weighting change the ideal setup position. Three visual references: address position, eye line, and ball position.
bonuses.objection_addressed: Will I need to change my setup? / How do I use this differently from my current putter?

bonuses.name: The Forward Axis Weighting Advantage — Digital Guide
bonuses.description: Short-form digital guide (PDF) explaining the science of Forward Axis Weighting, why CG position matters more than any other putter spec, and how to verify it yourself with the Face Down Balance test. Includes comparison framework for evaluating any putter's CG position.
bonuses.objection_addressed: Is this real science or just marketing? / How do I know this actually works? / How do I explain this to my golf buddies?

bonuses.name: Founders 500 Certificate of Authenticity
bonuses.description: Individually numbered certificate confirming the buyer's RS1 as part of the first 500 units produced. Signed by Chris McGinley. Includes serial number matching the putter head.
bonuses.objection_addressed: Is this a mass-market product or something special? / Am I getting a premium experience for a premium price?

enhancement_stack.bonuses_note: RS1 is a premium physical product sold via branded eCom — NOT a traditional DR info-product. The enhancement stack reflects premium product packaging, not inflated bonus stacking. No '$3,997 of FREE bonuses' energy. These are natural, expected components of a premium putter purchase, presented as thoughtful inclusions rather than value-stack props.

guarantee.language: Take the RS1 to the course. Play 3 rounds. If your putting doesn't feel measurably different — if you don't feel the face tracking square without your hands fighting it — return it for a full refund. No questions beyond confirming you gave it 3 rounds.
guarantee.conditions: Play 3 rounds with the RS1 within 30 days of delivery
guarantee.rationale: Conditional guarantee is appropriate for a physical product at this price point. The condition (3 rounds) is low-effort and actually helps the customer experience the physics in real conditions. The language avoids promising specific scoring outcomes (proof ceiling respected). The guarantee reverses the risk of a $399-429 purchase while protecting against returns from buyers who never used it.
guarantee.compliance_note: Must verify return logistics and restocking policy with PG operations team. Physical product guarantees have different cost implications than digital product guarantees.

scarcity.mechanism: Founders 500 Edition — first 500 units individually numbered with Certificate of Authenticity signed by Chris McGinley. Once the 500 are gone, future production runs will not carry the Founders designation, serial numbering, or signed certificate.
scarcity.language: Founders 500 Edition — 500 individually numbered RS1 putters. Each one signed by the designer. When they're gone, the Founders Edition is gone.
scarcity.enforcement: True limited production run. Serial numbers 001-500. Once sold, the Founders 500 designation, COA, and signed certificate are permanently discontinued. Future RS1 production will continue but without these elements.
scarcity.authenticity_note: This is real scarcity — a genuine limited production run with physical serial numbers. Not artificial countdown timers or fake urgency.

urgency.mechanism: Founders 500 launch event — once the initial allocation sells through, the product transitions to standard availability without Founders benefits. No artificial deadline — urgency is driven by finite inventory.
urgency.deadline: When the 500 units sell out (no specific date — inventory-driven)
urgency.language: The Founders 500 is a one-time production run. When the last one ships, the Founders Edition is gone. The RS1 will continue — but not with these numbers, not with this certificate, and not at this moment in the product's history.

price_architecture.anchor_price: $449-499 (Scotty Cameron Special Select $449-469, LAB Golf DF3/Mezz.1 $449-499)
price_architecture.discount_frame: RS1 is priced below both Scotty Cameron and LAB Golf — the two most prominent brands in the premium putter category. The golfer gets a provable physics advantage (Forward Axis Weighting) at a lower price than either competitor's comparable model.
price_architecture.price_justification: At $399, the RS1 costs less than a Scotty Cameron Special Select ($449-469) and less than a LAB Golf DF3 ($449-499). Scotty Cameron offers craftsmanship without mechanism innovation. LAB offers zero torque but not Forward Axis Weighting or technique assist. RS1 offers both — Forward Axis Weighting that eliminates face drift AND assists the natural stroke — at a lower price than either alternative. The question isn't whether $399 is expensive. It's whether it's worth solving a problem that has cost you 3-5 strokes per round for years.
comparison_anchors: Scotty Cameron Special Select: $449-469 — premium craftsmanship, no mechanism claim, no physics advantage
LAB Golf DF3: $449-499 — zero torque eliminates twist, but doesn't assist technique, acquired for $200M
TaylorMade Spider: $349-399 — high MOI (staleness 10/10), saturated claim
Odyssey Ai-ONE: $299-349 — AI face (staleness 6/10), saturated mechanism
Cost of the putter carousel: $1,000-3,000+ over 3-5 years on putters that all share the same design flaw (Rear Axis Weighting)

price_psychology.anchoring_strategy: Anchor against Scotty and LAB (both $449+), then reveal RS1 at $399. The gap ($50-100 less) plus the Forward Axis Weighting advantage creates immediate price-value clarity.
price_psychology.value_reframe: RS1 is cheaper than both premium competitors AND has a provable physics advantage neither can claim. The putter carousel typically costs $1,000-3,000+ over years — RS1 at $399 ends the carousel.
price_psychology.two_sku_strategy: Standard ($399) and Upgraded ($429) serve different buyer psychologies. The Standard buyer is value-conscious and wants the Forward Axis Weighting physics at the lowest entry point. The Upgraded buyer wants the complete premium experience (graphite shaft = lighter, PU grip = softer feel). The $30 gap is small enough that many buyers will upgrade, raising AOV.

formula_components.magnetic_reason: First 500 individually numbered by the designer
formula_components.avatar: Serious golfers who are better ball-strikers than putters
formula_components.time_interval: From the first putt (no adjustment period)

alternates: RS1 by Performance Golf — The Forward Axis Weighted Putter
The RS1 Putter — Do Less. Make More.
RS1 Founders Collection — Forward Axis Weighting by Chris McGinley
naming.naming_rationale: RS1 Founders 500 Edition is the launch name. 'Founders 500' creates scarcity and premium positioning. 'RS1' (Roll Straight 1) is the product name. The campaign uses 'Let it fall.' as the tagline and 'Gravity-Driven. Face-Controlled.' as the product truth line. No need for a separate 'offer name' — this is a premium branded product launch, not a DR offer package.

presentation_script.format_note: This is NOT a traditional DR presentation script. It is the offer section of a premium branded eCom page (Apple/VanMoof style). The D-F-W-B-P framework is adapted for scrolling product page sections rather than linear pitch.
presentation_script.core_product_intro: RS1. The first putter built with Forward Axis Weighting. 75% of the head weight sits in the front 25% of the putter. The face stays square because physics says it should.

deliverable_presentations.deliverable: RS1 Putter Head — Forward Axis Weighted
deliverable_presentations.feature: 360g multi-material construction with 75%+ of weight positioned ahead of the shaft axis
deliverable_presentations.why: Every other putter puts the weight behind the shaft, creating a gravitational force that drifts the face open. RS1 reverses that physics.
deliverable_presentations.benefit: The face tracks square through impact without hand manipulation. Your hands guide the stroke. Gravity handles the face.
deliverable_presentations.proof: Place it face-down on any flat surface. The face sits down. Every other putter face sits up. That's Face Down Balance — visible proof the weight is where it should be.

deliverable_presentations.feature: CNC-machined steel face, aluminum tail, carbon composite crown
deliverable_presentations.why: Extreme Forward Axis Weighting is impossible with single-material construction. Heavy material where weight matters (face), light material where it doesn't (crown, tail).
deliverable_presentations.benefit: The engineering precision of a racing instrument, not the mass-produced simplicity of a casting. Every gram is where Chris McGinley placed it.
deliverable_presentations.proof: Designed by Chris McGinley — 25+ years at major OEMs, clubs for 11 world #1 players. 10-piece construction vs. the 1-3 piece construction of conventional putters.

deliverable_presentations.feature: 4 degrees more upright than industry standard (~70 degrees)
deliverable_presentations.why: A more upright lie angle reduces the stroke arc, producing a more straight-back-straight-through motion. Less arc = less face rotation = less error.
deliverable_presentations.benefit: Double reduction in face drift: Forward Axis Weighting resists face opening, AND the upright lie angle reduces how much the face opens in the first place.
deliverable_presentations.proof: Measurable specification: 74 degrees vs. industry standard ~70 degrees. The geometry is built into the putter — it is not adjustable because it is optimized.

bonus_presentations.feature: Printed reference card showing optimal RS1 setup: address position, eye line, ball position
bonus_presentations.why: The upright lie angle changes the ideal setup slightly. This card ensures the transition is seamless.
bonus_presentations.benefit: Confidence from the first putt — no guesswork about how to set up with the new geometry.
bonus_presentations.proof: Developed from Chris McGinley's setup recommendations based on 25+ years of fitting world-class putters.

bonus_presentations.feature: Digital guide explaining CG position science and the Face Down Balance verification test
bonus_presentations.why: Educated buyers are confident buyers. Understanding WHY Forward Axis Weighting works builds trust that lasts.
bonus_presentations.benefit: Verify the physics yourself. Share the knowledge with playing partners who ask about your putter.
bonus_presentations.proof: Based on established physics principles (center of gravity, gravitational torque) — not proprietary marketing claims.

presentation_script.value_demonstration: If all the RS1 did was stop your hands from fighting the putter face on every stroke — would that be worth $399? If all it did was make your practice-green putting transfer to the course under pressure — would that change what you'd pay? If all it did was end the putter carousel, so this was the last putter you ever bought — how much is that worth over the next 5 years of putters you'd never need to buy? The RS1 does all three. And it costs less than a Scotty Cameron.

price_reveal.anchor: Scotty Cameron Special Select: $449-469. LAB Golf DF3: $449-499. Both premium. Neither has Forward Axis Weighting.
price_reveal.actual: RS1 Standard: $399. RS1 Upgraded (graphite shaft, PU grip): $429.
price_reveal.savings: $50-100 less than either premium competitor — with a physics advantage neither can claim.

presentation_script.risk_reversal: Take the RS1 to the course. Play 3 rounds. Feel the face track square without your hands fighting it. If you don't feel the difference — if your putting doesn't feel measurably different — send it back. Full refund. We only ask that you give it 3 rounds. That's the minimum to feel what Forward Axis Weighting changes.
cta_sequence: Choose your RS1: Standard ($399) or Upgraded ($429). Founders 500 — individually numbered.
Secure your Founders number. 500 putters. When they're gone, the Founders Edition is gone.
The weight has been in the wrong place your entire putting life. Move it forward. Let it fall.
presentation_script.consequence_amplification: Every round you play with a rear-axis-weighted putter, the face drifts open the same way it always has. The same pushes. The same anxiety over 3-footers. The same gap between your ball-striking and your score. The physics haven't changed since the first day you picked up a putter — and they won't change until the weight moves forward.

validation_scores.differentiation_notes: RS1 is the only premium putter with Forward Axis Weighting and visual proof (FDB). Scotty Cameron has craftsmanship, LAB has zero torque, TaylorMade has MOI — none has forward CG with face-down balance demonstration. Price undercuts both premium competitors.
validation_scores.promise_alignment_notes: The offer delivers precisely what the promise claims: a putter that works with gravity (Forward Axis Weighting), requires less effort (natural pendulum), and produces putting that matches ball-striking (face stays square under pressure). Every offer element traces directly to the primary promise 'Do less. Make more.'
validation_scores.anti_slop_notes: Zero generic Hormozi language in final output. No inflated value stacking. No 'game-changing' or 'revolutionary' language. All claims grounded in physics and engineering specs. Voice register matches Soul.md (quiet power + earned authority).

arena_results.arena_format: 5 competitors x 2 rounds (condensed per user instruction)
arena_results.selection_rationale: Premium branded eCom architecture won because RS1 is a physical product launching into the premium putter category ($399-429). Traditional DR offer stacking (inflated bonus values, 12-bonus format, video training add-ons) would undermine the Apple/VanMoof premium positioning and violate Soul.md voice constraints. The winning architecture treats the putter as an engineered instrument, not an info-product wrapped in bonuses.

to_vsl.note: RS1 uses premium branded eCom page format, NOT traditional VSL. However, if video content is produced:
to_vsl.offer_section_script: Present the two SKUs ($399/$429), anchor against Scotty Cameron and LAB Golf pricing, reveal Founders 500 limited edition, 30-day 3-round conditional guarantee
to_vsl.price_reveal_sequence: Anchor (Scotty $449-469, LAB $449-499) → RS1 reveal ($399/$429) → savings frame ($50-100 less with Forward Axis Weighting advantage) → Founders 500 scarcity → guarantee
to_vsl.emotional_close: Move the weight forward. The fight is over. The arrow is finally straight. Let it fall.
to_vsl.cta_structure: Choose your RS1 → Secure your Founders number → The weight has been in the wrong place your entire putting life. Move it forward.

to_email.launch_sequence_structure: Email 1: The Bent Arrow tease (root cause hook). Email 2: Forward Axis Weighting reveal (mechanism). Email 3: Face Down Balance demo (proof). Email 4: Do less. Make more. (promise). Email 5: Founders 500 announcement (offer). Email 6: Scarcity (inventory update). Email 7: Consequence amplification (last chance).
to_email.offer_email_elements: Two SKUs ($399/$429), Founders 500 individually numbered, Chris McGinley signed COA, 30-day 3-round guarantee, price anchoring vs Scotty Cameron and LAB Golf
subject_line_angles: The first 500 are numbered. Here's why that matters.
Less than a Scotty. More physics than a LAB.
3 rounds. If you don't feel the difference, send it back.

to_landing_page.page_format: Premium branded eCom (Apple/VanMoof style). Scrolling sections with generous whitespace. Hero image + headline. Problem section. Mechanism section with demo visual. Proof section. Specs section. Offer section with two SKUs. Guarantee. Founders 500 section. CTA.
to_landing_page.offer_section_requirements: Two SKU cards (Standard $399 / Upgraded $429) with clear feature differentiation. Founders 500 badge with serial number preview. 30-day guarantee badge. Price comparison to Scotty Cameron and LAB Golf. Single CTA button per SKU.
to_landing_page.key_visual_requirements: RS1 hero product shot, Face Down Balance comparison photo (RS1 vs competitor), 10-piece construction exploded view, Chris McGinley portrait, Founders 500 certificate mockup
to_landing_page.copy_integration: All headlines from big-idea-output.json and promise-output.json are pre-approved for page use. Tagline ('Let it fall.') appears in hero and close sections. Product truth ('Gravity-Driven. Face-Controlled.') appears as section header.
